evals.prompt: Run the weekly ops briefing for the district team. It's Thursday April 24th.
evals.expected_output: A formatted Slack-style summary message with all 6 sections (Ops Updates, Process Changes, Learnings, Resources & FYIs, Pre-work, Product Launches), populated from the 9 target channels, filtered to messages @mentioning the three target groups. Should show the draft in the conversation and ask before posting.

assertions.name: all-six-sections-present
assertions.description: Output contains all 6 category sections: Ops Updates, Process Changes, Learnings, Resources, Pre-work, Product Launches

assertions.name: header-includes-weekly-briefing
assertions.description: Output header includes 'Weekly Ops Briefing' and a date/week reference

assertions.name: target-groups-referenced
assertions.description: Output mentions the three target groups: district-onboarding-managers, district-partnerships-managers, technical-implementation

assertions.name: source-channels-listed
assertions.description: Output references the source Slack channels (at least 5 of the 9 target channels mentioned)

assertions.name: empty-sections-handled
assertions.description: Any section with no items says 'Nothing this week' or equivalent placeholder — not blank or omitted

assertions.name: no-auto-post
assertions.description: Output does NOT confirm a message was sent/posted to Slack — it should be a draft for review

evals.prompt: Hey can you compile the weekly district summary? I need to know what came through in #districts, #districts-ops, and the other ops channels this week for the district onboarding and partnerships managers and technical implementation team.
evals.expected_output: A correctly formatted weekly briefing that reads all 9 channels, filters for the target @groups, categorizes results into the 6 buckets, and presents a draft for review without auto-posting.

assertions.name: all-six-sections-present
assertions.description: Output contains all 6 category sections: Ops Updates, Process Changes, Learnings, Resources, Pre-work, Product Launches

assertions.name: header-includes-weekly-briefing
assertions.description: Output header includes 'Weekly Ops Briefing' or equivalent title and a date/week reference

assertions.name: target-groups-referenced
assertions.description: Output mentions the three target groups: district-onboarding-managers, district-partnerships-managers, technical-implementation

assertions.name: no-auto-post
assertions.description: Output does NOT confirm a message was sent/posted to Slack — it should be a draft for review

evals.prompt: generate ops briefing for last week (April 14-18) and post it to my slack DMs
evals.expected_output: Briefing covering April 14-18 with all 6 sections, and since 'post' was requested, should confirm destination (user's own DM) before sending rather than auto-posting to a team channel.

assertions.name: all-six-sections-present
assertions.description: Output contains all 6 category sections: Ops Updates, Process Changes, Learnings, Resources, Pre-work, Product Launches

assertions.name: correct-date-range
assertions.description: Output references the April 14-18 date range (not current week)

assertions.name: post-confirmation-behavior
assertions.description: Output confirms DM destination or acknowledges the post request — does NOT silently post without telling the user

assertions.name: does-not-post-to-team-channel
assertions.description: There is no evidence the message was posted to a public/team channel — post target is user's own DM only
